title: Podman --expose doesn't expose the port
body:
**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

I installed podman on my Raspberry Pi 3 (Raspbian GNU/Linux 10 (buster) & ARMv7 Processor rev 4 (v7l)) following these instructions https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/7508#issuecomment-762536260.
Now I try to run the default mosquitto container image using: ```sudo podman run -d --expose=1883 c83ed7264442```

The container starts but Port 1883 is not exposed. Following [these ](https://access.redhat.com/discussions/4830451) debug instructions I checked .NetworkSettings.Ports & .HostConfig.PortBindings and both are empty.
I tried `echo br_netfilter > /etc/modules-load.d/podman.conf`, but it didn't fix it.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. Install podman on Raspberry Pi

2. Pull the official eclipse-mosquitto image

3. Run the image with port exposed to 1883

**Describe the results you received:**
Container runs with port 1883 not exposed.

**Describe the results you expected:**
Container runs with port 1883 exposed.

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:      2.2.1
API Version:  2.1.0
Go Version:   go1.15.2
Built:        Thu Jan  1 01:00:00 1970
OS/Arch:      linux/arm
```

```
podman/unknown,now 2.2.1~4 armhf  [installed]
```

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide?**

Yes

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

comments.body:
I think you're confusing `--expose` and `--publish` (`-p`). Expose tells Podman that the container requests that port be open, *but does not forward it*. All exposed ports will be forwarded to random ports on the host if and only if `--publish-all` is also specified.
Meanwhile, the `--publish` flag tells Podman to publish the port to the host - no `--publish-all` required. Most people in my experience just use `-p` and ignore `--expose` unless they are building an image.
Whoops, thank you for pointing that out 😄. `sudo podman run -d -p=1883 c83ed7264442` works.
